title: Dolicholus pringlei
intro: Dolicholus pringlei, also known as Mexican Pringle's dolicholus, is a perennial herbaceous plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and exhibiting antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Dolicholus pringlei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant has been used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into folk remedies for various health issues.
taxonomy: Dolicholus pringlei is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It is native to Mexico. The classification of Dolicholus pringlei includes being a member of the genus Dolicholus. The genus Dolicholus is part of the larger family Boraginaceae. Dolicholus pringlei is further classified as a part of the order Boraginales.
